name: seewav
description: Audio waveform visualisation, converts any audio to a nice video
readme_summary: Seewav is a Python tool that generates animated videos from audio files by visualizing their waveforms. It allows users to customize various aspects of the animation, such as colors, resolution, and playback speed, to create engaging visual representations of sound.
key_features: Generates MP4 animations from audio files
Customizable video framerate
Support for stereo audio visualization
Adjustable bar colors and background color
Configurable number of bars, oversampling, and time display
Control over animation speed, width, and height
Seek and duration options for specific parts of the audio
primary_use_case: Creating animated video visualizations of audio waveforms for content creators, musicians, or anyone wanting to visually represent sound.
cover_image_prompt: A sound engineer in a futuristic studio, holding a glowing audio cable that streams vibrant, pulsating sound waves. These waves are being sculpted by the engineer's hands into a dynamic, colorful waveform animation that flows onto a large screen behind them, displaying the 'SeeWav' logo subtly. The input is the raw audio cable, and the output is the mesmerizing visual animation. The scene is illuminated by the glow of the sound waves and studio equipment, with subtle digital particles and UI elements floating around. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a dark, high-tech aesthetic and vibrant, contrasting colors for the waveforms.

name: cryptgeon
description: cryptgeon is a secure, open source note / file sharing service inspired by PrivNote written in rust & svelte.
readme_summary: Cryptgeon is an open-source, secure note and file sharing service inspired by PrivNote. It offers client-side encryption, ensuring the server cannot access the content, and supports self-destructing notes with view or time limits. It includes a web interface, a CLI client, and a Raycast extension.
key_features: Send text or files
Client-side encryption (server cannot decrypt)
View or time constraints (self-destructing)
In-memory storage (no persistence)
Obligatory dark mode support
Web service, CLI client, and Raycast extension
primary_use_case: Securely sharing sensitive text notes or files that automatically disappear after being viewed or after a set time, without the server having access to the content.
cover_image_prompt: A minimalist, modern scene depicting a secure digital vault opening to reveal a single, glowing document. A stylized, abstract 'key' made of digital particles is shown being inserted into the vault's lock, symbolizing client-side encryption. The document inside is protected by a faint, shimmering shield, indicating its ephemeral nature. Subtle UI elements like a countdown timer and a 'viewed' icon are visible on the vault's surface. The overall mood is secure and ephemeral, with a clean, dark background and glowing highlights. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear iconography and a sophisticated, tech-inspired color palette.

name: sparkle
description: A Windows app to debloat and optimize your PC
readme_summary: Sparkle is a Windows application built with React and Electron, designed to debloat and optimize your PC. It offers features for applying system tweaks, managing temporary files, integrating with Winget for app installations, and backing up/reverting changes.
key_features: Apply Tweaks to Optimize your system
Manage All Temp files in one place
Install apps with the built-in Winget integration
Backup and Revert changes
View Basic System info
primary_use_case: Debloating and optimizing a Windows PC through a user-friendly application interface.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, friendly robot character is meticulously cleaning and organizing a cluttered digital cityscape representing a Windows PC. The robot is using a glowing wrench to tighten loose digital gears and a digital vacuum to suck up stray 'temp file' particles. On one side, a digital 'app store' icon with a checkmark indicates new apps being neatly installed. On the other side, a 'backup' button is prominently displayed, with a digital shield protecting the cityscape. The overall mood is clean, efficient, and futuristic, with a bright, optimistic color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with smooth, rounded edges and subtle glowing highlights.

name: torrra
description: A Python tool that lets you search and download torrents without leaving your CLI.
readme_summary: Torrra is a Python-based command-line tool that provides a text-based user interface (TUI) for searching and downloading torrents. It integrates with indexers like Jackett and Prowlarr, leverages libtorrent for downloads, and is built using the Textual framework for a rich terminal experience.
key_features: Search and download torrents from the CLI
Textual-based TUI for an interactive experience
Integration with Jackett and Prowlarr
Pause/resume download support
Customizable themes
Native support for Linux, macOS, and Windows
primary_use_case: Searching and downloading torrents directly from the command line with a user-friendly TUI.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot sitting at a sleek, futuristic command-line terminal. The robot is holding a glowing magnifying glass over a digital map of the internet, with data streams flowing from various points towards the terminal. On the terminal screen, a torrent client interface is visible, showing download progress bars and file names. Subtle digital particles and code snippets float around the robot and terminal, indicating the software's function. The overall scene is clean, modern, and slightly whimsical, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a cool blue and green color palette, clearly depicting the seamless search and download of torrents within a terminal environment. The repository name 'Torrra' is subtly integrated into a UI element on the terminal.

name: gamewiki
description: An intelligent Wiki overlay tool designed specifically for gamers, featuring GEMINI-powered RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) capabilities for smart Q&A services.
readme_summary: GameWiki is an AI-powered in-game overlay tool for gamers, providing instant access to wiki information and smart Q&A services powered by Google Gemini's RAG capabilities. It allows users to get answers and guides without leaving their game, enhancing the gaming experience.
key_features: One Hotkey for instant overlay access (Ctrl+Q)
AI Game Expert powered by Google Gemini with local knowledge bases
Real-time wiki overlay
Supports over 100 games for wiki access
AI-enhanced knowledge bases for specific games (HELLDIVERS 2, Elden Ring, Don't Starve Together, Civilization VI)
Web browser functionality within the overlay
Customizable hotkeys and settings
primary_use_case: To provide gamers with an unobtrusive, AI-driven overlay for quick access to game information, guides, and answers without interrupting gameplay.
cover_image_prompt: A 3D isometric illustration of a gamer deeply engrossed in a vibrant fantasy video game on their monitor. Emerging from the monitor, a translucent, glowing overlay screen hovers beside the gamer's head, displaying helpful game tips and an AI chatbot interface. The overlay is subtly integrated, like a helpful spirit guiding the player. Small, stylized icons representing game items and characters float around the overlay. The overall mood is one of seamless integration and enhanced gameplay, with the GameWiki logo subtly visible on the overlay. The scene uses a cool, futuristic color palette with glowing accents.
